From round 1 · F3 · now built

PDP Fit Confidence — Before → After

14,744 reviews hold the fit answer, but the buy box shows none of it. This surfaces a "true to size" bar mined from reviews, model height on the image, and a working size finder — right where the size decision happens. Try the finder and the size hints.

📱 Live site · actual screenshot · White Cotton Pant PDP
·Size buttons show only waist inches — no "true to size" signal from the 14,744 reviews.
·Product images carry no model height or size-worn reference.
·No "find my size" helper — the shopper is left to guess and hope.

Round 1 · F3 · built as prototype

The fit answer exists — it's just in the wrong place

Issue

No model height on images. No "true to size" signal near the size picker. The data lives in 14,744 reviews — it's simply not surfaced where the decision happens.

Why it's worth building, not just slotting

Size doubt is the #1 cause of no-add in Indian apparel and the top driver of returns & RTO. RTO doesn't just cost the refund — it burns forward + reverse shipping on an order that looked "converted". Fixing fit confidence protects margin on orders you already win.

What the prototype adds

(1) A true-to-size distribution bar mined from review text · (2) model height + size worn on the image · (3) a "Find my size" recommender that maps waist → size. All three feed off data SAADAA already owns.

Hypothesis: surfacing review-based fit confidence + model height + a size finder at the buy box reduces size hesitation → ↑ add-to-cart, ↓ return rate, ↓ RTO. Primary metric: ATC rate. Guardrails: return rate & RTO (the real prize — watch these for 4–6 weeks post-ship, not just day-1 CR).
Effort · Low–Med Impact · High Uses data you own

Proof: Bombay Shirt Co fit guarantee · Uniqlo MySize · Everlane fit descriptors · Amazon "fit: true to size" bar from reviews.
